verses.text: Word comes that the Philistines are robbing Keilah, and David asks God whether to fight. His own men are afraid, yet his first move is to enquire of the LORD. Before David acts, he asks — the very habit Saul abandoned. The godly bring even their bold plans to God.

Word comes that the Philistines are robbing Keilah, and David asks God whether to fight. His own men are afraid, yet his first move is to enquire of the LORD. Before David acts, he asks — the very habit Saul abandoned. The godly bring even their bold plans to God.

Word comes that the Philistines are robbing Keilah, and David asks God whether to fight. His own men are afraid, yet his first move is to enquire of the LORD. Before David acts, he asks — the very habit Saul abandoned. The godly bring even their bold plans to God.

When his men hesitate, David asks the LORD a second time, and with God's clear word goes and saves the city. He does not lean on one answer or his own resolve, but seeks fresh assurance and then obeys fully. Confirmed by God, he acts without fear.

When his men hesitate, David asks the LORD a second time, and with God's clear word goes and saves the city. He does not lean on one answer or his own resolve, but seeks fresh assurance and then obeys fully. Confirmed by God, he acts without fear.

Saul hears that David is shut up in a walled town and rejoices: 'God hath delivered him into mine hand.' He reads providence to flatter his own purpose. Men often mistake their opportunity for God's blessing, calling their chance to sin a gift from heaven.

Saul hears that David is shut up in a walled town and rejoices: 'God hath delivered him into mine hand.' He reads providence to flatter his own purpose. Men often mistake their opportunity for God's blessing, calling their chance to sin a gift from heaven.

Saul hears that David is shut up in a walled town and rejoices: 'God hath delivered him into mine hand.' He reads providence to flatter his own purpose. Men often mistake their opportunity for God's blessing, calling their chance to sin a gift from heaven.

Sensing the trap, David asks God through the ephod whether the men of Keilah will betray him — and they will, even after he saved them. Forewarned, he slips away. God answers honest questions honestly, and the gratitude of men is a frail thing to lean on.

David and his six hundred move through the wilderness strongholds, and 'Saul sought him every day, but God delivered him not into his hand.' The hunt is relentless, yet so is the keeping. No daily search can catch the man God has daily resolved to protect.

David and his six hundred move through the wilderness strongholds, and 'Saul sought him every day, but God delivered him not into his hand.' The hunt is relentless, yet so is the keeping. No daily search can catch the man God has daily resolved to protect.

Jonathan finds David in the woods and 'strengthened his hand in God,' assuring him he will yet be king. A true friend comes not to fix our troubles but to point us back to God. Jonathan's last gift to David is courage drawn from the LORD's promise.

The men of Ziph go to Saul and offer to hand David over, eager to win the king's favour by betrayal. There are always those who will sell the innocent for a great man's smile. Yet their plot only sets the stage for another of God's deliverances.

Saul closes in until only a mountain stands between him and David, and David's capture seems certain — Saul on one side, David fleeing on the other. The net is drawn tight. At the very edge of disaster, God's people learn how thin the margin is, and how sure His hand.

Saul closes in until only a mountain stands between him and David, and David's capture seems certain — Saul on one side, David fleeing on the other. The net is drawn tight. At the very edge of disaster, God's people learn how thin the margin is, and how sure His hand.

At that moment a messenger calls Saul away to meet a Philistine raid, and David escapes. They name the place the rock of parting. When help seems impossible, God moves an enemy far off to rescue His servant. His timing is exact, His deliverance complete.
